developer_profile.platform: macOS first, with optional iOS companion
developer_profile.assumptions: Inferred from current repository context because profile questions were skipped.

shortlist.one_liner: macOS app that routes pasted prompts to the right AI model and keeps local, searchable run history for terminal-heavy developers.
shortlist.problem_statement: Developers lose time picking models and rewriting prompts across tools. Existing options are either web-heavy or expensive, and they do not fit terminal-native workflows.
shortlist.target_user: Indie developers and engineers who use terminal + AI daily

feasibility.solo_dev_scope: EXCELLENT (3-5 weeks - local app, no mandatory backend)
feasibility.platform_api_fit: STRONG (AppKit/SwiftUI, Shortcuts, clipboard monitoring, local storage)
feasibility.monetization_viability: STRONG (one-time Pro unlock or subscription for provider integrations)
feasibility.competition_density: STRONG (crowded broadly, but terminal-first niche remains fragmented)

shortlist.monetization_model: Freemium - free local routing and history, paid integrations/templates/workspaces
shortlist.competition_notes: General AI clients are crowded, but many complaints focus on price and poor workflow fit for developers.
shortlist.mvp_scope: Prompt input, model presets, local history with tags, one-click rerun, shell command snippet export
shortlist.next_step: product-agent discover --idea "macOS app that routes developer prompts to the right AI model and keeps local searchable run history for terminal workflows" --platform macOS --output-format json

shortlist.one_liner: A distraction-light coding focus timer with Git/terminal-aware milestones and automatic session notes.
shortlist.problem_statement: Pomodoro apps are generic and disconnected from real coding progress. Developers want lightweight focus support tied to commits and terminal activity.
shortlist.target_user: Software developers who time-block deep work

feasibility.platform_api_fit: STRONG (menu bar app, notifications, optional git integration)
feasibility.monetization_viability: MODERATE (small ticket one-time app or bundle upsell)
feasibility.technical_fit: EXCELLENT (strong fit for local macOS utility development)

shortlist.monetization_model: Paid upfront ($9-19) with optional Pro analytics pack
shortlist.competition_notes: Category is crowded, but user complaints often mention overcomplex UX and subscription fatigue.
shortlist.mvp_scope: Menu bar timer, quick goals, optional git event markers, end-session summary note
shortlist.next_step: product-agent discover --idea "Menu bar focus timer for developers that tracks coding sessions with git and terminal-aware milestones" --platform macOS --output-format json

shortlist.idea: On-Device Commit and PR Draft Assistant
shortlist.one_liner: Use Apple Foundation Models on-device to draft commit messages and PR summaries from diffs without sending code to cloud APIs.
shortlist.problem_statement: Teams want AI writing help for commits and PRs but often cannot upload private code to third-party services.
shortlist.target_user: Privacy-conscious developers and teams on Apple Silicon Macs

feasibility.solo_dev_scope: STRONG (5-7 weeks - local AI integration plus git parsing)
feasibility.platform_api_fit: EXCELLENT (Foundation Models, local file/git context, Apple silicon optimization)
feasibility.monetization_viability: STRONG (clear B2C Pro pricing, possible team license later)
feasibility.technical_fit: STRONG (requires some new AI framework learning but strong core fit)

shortlist.monetization_model: Paid app + optional team pack (per-seat) with policy controls
shortlist.competition_notes: Cloud-based coding assistants dominate; privacy-first local positioning is a differentiator.
shortlist.mvp_scope: Select git diff, generate commit title/body options, generate PR summary + test checklist
shortlist.next_step: product-agent discover --idea "Privacy-first macOS assistant that uses on-device Apple Foundation Models to draft commit messages and PR summaries from git diffs" --platform macOS --output-format json

shortlist.one_liner: A sensory-light, keyboard-first task app for developers with time-blindness cues and tiny actionable next steps.
shortlist.problem_statement: Many productivity apps are visually noisy and generic. Neurodivergent developers often need simpler, low-friction interfaces and stronger pacing cues.

feasibility.solo_dev_scope: STRONG (6-8 weeks - phased macOS then iOS companion)
feasibility.platform_api_fit: STRONG (notifications, widgets, shortcuts, local-first sync options)
feasibility.monetization_viability: STRONG (high willingness to pay for fit and reliability)
feasibility.competition_density: STRONG (demand exists; many current options poorly matched)

shortlist.monetization_model: Freemium with subscription for advanced routines and cross-device sync
shortlist.competition_notes: Review patterns frequently mention pricing frustration, complexity, and poor UX consistency.
shortlist.mvp_scope: Fast capture, next-action decomposition, focus cues, recurring routines, simple weekly review
shortlist.next_step: product-agent discover --idea "Keyboard-first ADHD-friendly task app for developers with time-blindness cues and low-friction next actions" --platform "macOS + iOS" --output-format json

ideas_filtered_out.reason: Failed solo_dev_scope filter. Too broad and highly competitive for a 4-8 week solo MVP.
Failed technical_fit and scope filters due to backend and ops complexity.
Failed competition_density filter. Overcrowded category with weak differentiation.

recommendation: Start with Rank 1 (Prompt Router for Terminal Workflows). It best matches your current strengths, can ship quickly as a solo local-first product, and has a clean monetization path without requiring heavy infrastructure. Run the next_step command to validate demand before building.
